evidence.title: Indian Express May 23 report - OIIX A1010/26 and eight-airport limits
evidence.source_description: Indian Express article text retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-20 UTC
evidence.relevance: Independent corroborating report that identifies NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, western Tehran FIR shutdown effective May 22 to May 25, suspension of civil permits, eight operational airports, daylight-only operations, per-flight approval, and rerouting.

evidence.title: Abdi Media NOTAM A1010/26 extract
evidence.source_description: Abdi Media English article text retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-20 UTC
evidence.relevance: Provides specific NOTAM code A1010/26, validity dates, listed exceptions, cancellation of previous permissions, and need for new Civil Aviation Organization authorization for IFR passenger flights.

evidence.title: Gulf News May 24 live page extract - Iran restricts western airspace NOTAM
evidence.source_description: Gulf News live/story page text retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-20 UTC
evidence.relevance: Corroborates that Iran Civil Aviation Authority issued a NOTAM restricting operations at most western airports in Tehran FIR until early May 25, with limited daytime flights at eight sites; supports official/Iran-initiated, non-weather, pre-deadline restriction.

evidence.title: Gulf News May 23 report - Iran shuts western airspace
evidence.source_description: Gulf News article page text retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-20 UTC
evidence.relevance: Credible reporting that Iran Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary closure of western Iran airspace and suspended civil flight permits in the western sector of Tehran FIR, with only eight airports operating under daylight and per-flight approval restrictions.

evidence.title: Notamify raw NOTAM A1010/26 - airport operations closure text
evidence.source_description: Notamify OIIX NOTAM A1010/26 page text retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-20 UTC
evidence.relevance: Provides raw NOTAM text and classification for A1010/26. It identifies the notice as AD Airport Operations/QFALC, states all airports in the west part of Tehran FIR are closed except listed airports, and preserves the limited sunrise-sunset and new CAA permission language relevant to whether the event was an airspace/transit closure or an airport-operations restriction.

evidence.title: FAA International NOTAM Q-code table extract - QFA aerodrome and LC closed
evidence.source_description: FAA Order 7930.2 Appendix B International NOTAM (Q) Codes page retrieved by web.open/find on 2026-06-20 UTC
evidence.relevance: Helps interpret the raw NOTAM code QFALC in A1010/26. The FAA international NOTAM Q-code table states that second/third letters identify the subject, FA means Aerodrome, fourth/fifth letters identify operational status, and LC means Closed; it also distinguishes airspace subjects such as AF Flight Information Region and AR ATS route.
